metadata.name: Harry Potter
metadata.description: Questions spanning all seven books and eight films — spells, characters, magical creatures, Hogwarts lore, Quidditch, and the wizarding world.
metadata.author: Trivial World Team

questions.questionText: What is the name of the magical shopping street in London where wizards buy their school supplies?
questions.tidbits: Diagon Alley is accessed through the Leaky Cauldron pub on Charing Cross Road — invisible to Muggles.

questions.questionText: From which platform at King's Cross Station do Hogwarts students depart on the Hogwarts Express?
questions.tidbits: Students reach the platform by walking directly through the barrier between platforms 9 and 10.

questions.questionText: In which country is Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry located?
questions.tidbits: Hogwarts is hidden from Muggles by enchantments that make it appear as a dangerous ruin.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the wizarding village near Hogwarts that students are allowed to visit in their third year?
questions.tidbits: Hogsmeade is the only all-wizarding village in Britain. Its most popular establishments include the Three Broomsticks and Honeydukes.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the wizarding prison guarded by Dementors, located in the North Sea?
questions.tidbits: Prisoners in Azkaban are kept in a state of perpetual despair as Dementors feed on happy memories.

questions.questionText: In which village did Harry Potter grow up with his aunt and uncle?
questions.tidbits: Harry lived at 4 Privet Drive, Little Whinging — a perfectly ordinary-looking address for a most extraordinary boy.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the village where Harry Potter was born and where his parents were killed?
questions.tidbits: Godric's Hollow is also the birthplace of Godric Gryffindor and the home of Bathilda Bagshot.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the forest bordering Hogwarts that is strictly off-limits to students?
questions.tidbits: The Forbidden Forest is home to centaurs, unicorns, Acromantulas, and many other magical creatures.

questions.questionText: On which floor of the Ministry of Magic is the Department of Mysteries located?
questions.tidbits: The Department of Mysteries houses rooms studying time, space, thought, death, love, and prophecy.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the Weasley family's home, famous for its disheveled, magical appearance?
questions.tidbits: The Burrow appears to defy the laws of physics, held together largely by magic.

questions.questionText: Which actor played Harry Potter in all eight films?
questions.tidbits: Daniel Radcliffe was just 11 years old when he was cast as Harry Potter in 2000.

questions.questionText: Which actress played Hermione Granger in the Harry Potter films?
questions.tidbits: Emma Watson became a UN Women Goodwill Ambassador in 2014, championing gender equality.

questions.questionText: What is Voldemort's real birth name?
questions.tidbits: Tom Marvolo Riddle is an anagram of 'I am Lord Voldemort,' revealed when he rearranges letters with his wand.

questions.questionText: Which actor, known for his portrayal of the complex Severus Snape, passed away in January 2016?
questions.tidbits: J.K. Rowling privately told Alan Rickman Snape's true loyalty early on so he could play the character with the right layers.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the loyal house-elf who serves the Malfoy family before being freed by Harry?
questions.tidbits: Dobby is freed when Lucius Malfoy unknowingly presents him with a sock hidden inside Harry's diary.

questions.questionText: Which character is known as 'The Boy Who Lived' for surviving Voldemort's killing curse as an infant?
questions.tidbits: The love sacrifice of Harry's mother Lily created ancient magic that protected Harry from Voldemort's curse.

questions.questionText: What is the name of Draco Malfoy's mother, a Black family member who ultimately saves Harry's life in the forest?
questions.tidbits: Narcissa lies to Voldemort about Harry being dead, prioritizing her son Draco's safety over loyalty to the Dark Lord.

questions.questionText: What is the full name of the professor who taught Defense Against the Dark Arts in Harry's second year and claimed to be a famous author of DADA books?
questions.tidbits: Lockhart stole the achievements of other wizards, then erased their memories — a skill that ultimately backfired on him.

questions.questionText: Which actress played Bellatrix Lestrange in the Harry Potter films?
questions.tidbits: Bellatrix Lestrange is the most devoted Death Eater and a cousin of Sirius Black.

questions.questionText: What is Luna Lovegood's father's name, and what magazine does he publish?
questions.tidbits: The Quibbler publishes outlandish conspiracy theories but ironically breaks the true story of Voldemort's return.

questions.questionText: How many books are in the main Harry Potter series?
questions.tidbits: The seven books were published from 1997 to 2007 and have sold over 600 million copies worldwide.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the climactic battle that takes place at Hogwarts in the final book?
questions.tidbits: The Battle of Hogwarts takes place on May 2, 1998 — now celebrated as Harry Potter Day.

questions.questionText: In which book does Harry Potter first compete in the Triwizard Tournament?
questions.answerText: Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire (book 4)
questions.tidbits: The Triwizard Tournament is a prestigious inter-school competition held between Hogwarts, Beauxbatons, and Durmstrang.

questions.questionText: In which year does Dumbledore destroy one of Voldemort's Horcruxes — the Gaunt family ring — but is cursed in the process?
questions.answerText: Between books 5 and 6 (the summer before Harry's sixth year)
questions.tidbits: Dumbledore knew destroying the ring's curse would eventually kill him, making his pact with Snape all the more poignant.

questions.questionText: Who opens the Chamber of Secrets in Harry's second year by using a basilisk to attack Muggle-born students?
questions.answerText: Ginny Weasley (controlled by Riddle's diary Horcrux)
questions.tidbits: Tom Riddle's diary was Voldemort's first Horcrux, created when he was just 16 years old.

questions.questionText: What object does Harry use to travel back in time to save Sirius Black and Buckbeak in book three?
questions.tidbits: Hermione had been using the Time-Turner all year to attend multiple classes simultaneously.

questions.questionText: During the Triwizard Tournament, what are the three tasks the champions must complete?
questions.answerText: Steal a dragon's egg, rescue hostages from the lake, navigate a maze
questions.tidbits: Harry was the only champion to rescue all hostages from the Black Lake, demonstrating his moral character.

questions.questionText: Which four Horcruxes are destroyed before Harry, Ron, and Hermione begin their hunt in book seven?
questions.answerText: Tom Riddle's diary, Marvolo Gaunt's ring, Salazar Slytherin's locket, Helga Hufflepuff's cup
questions.tidbits: The locket is destroyed by Ron using the Sword of Gryffindor, overcoming the Horcrux's psychological attack.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the prophecy that links Harry Potter and Voldemort, and who first heard it?
questions.answerText: The Prophecy of the Chosen One; Professor Trelawney (overheard by Snape)
questions.tidbits: Because Snape only heard part of the prophecy before being caught, Voldemort chose Harry — not Neville Longbottom — as his target.

questions.questionText: In what order were the Deathly Hallows originally owned, according to 'The Tale of the Three Brothers'?
questions.answerText: Elder Wand (first brother), Resurrection Stone (second brother), Cloak of Invisibility (third brother)
questions.tidbits: Death himself is said to have gifted the Hallows to three brothers who outwitted him at a dangerous bridge.

questions.questionText: What spell is used to disarm an opponent in wizard duels, and is Harry's signature spell?
questions.tidbits: Harry's repeated use of Expelliarmus instead of Avada Kedavra reflects his moral refusal to kill.

questions.questionText: What spell conjures a Patronus to repel Dementors?
questions.tidbits: A Patronus takes the form of an animal that reflects the caster's innermost character.

questions.questionText: What are the three Unforgivable Curses — the Killing Curse, the Cruciatus Curse, and what third curse?
questions.tidbits: During Voldemort's first reign, many Death Eaters claimed to have been under the Imperius Curse to escape prosecution.

questions.questionText: What are the three objects that make up the Deathly Hallows?
questions.answerText: The Elder Wand, the Resurrection Stone, and the Cloak of Invisibility
questions.tidbits: The one who unites all three Deathly Hallows is said to become the Master of Death.

questions.questionText: What magical document, created by the four Marauders, shows every person's location within Hogwarts at any moment?
questions.tidbits: The map must be activated by saying 'I solemnly swear that I am up to no good' and cleared with 'Mischief managed.'

questions.questionText: What spell unlocks doors?
questions.tidbits: Alohomora comes from the Manga Ewe language of West Africa, meaning 'friendly to thieves.'

questions.questionText: What spell produces light from the tip of a wand?
questions.tidbits: Its counter-spell, Nox, extinguishes the light. The incantation derives from the Latin word for light.

questions.questionText: What are the seven objects Voldemort chose to make into Horcruxes — six intentional and one accidental?
questions.answerText: Tom Riddle's diary, Marvolo Gaunt's ring, Slytherin's locket, Hufflepuff's cup, Ravenclaw's diadem, the snake Nagini, and Harry Potter (accidental)
questions.tidbits: Voldemort intended to split his soul into seven pieces, considering seven the most magically powerful number.

questions.questionText: What spell is used to transform an object into something else entirely, one of the most complex spells taught at Hogwarts?
questions.answerText: Transfiguration (not a single incantation — it is a discipline, e.g. Vera Verto)
questions.tidbits: Transfiguration is considered one of the most difficult and dangerous branches of magic taught at Hogwarts.

questions.questionText: What charm, used on the trio's tent and the Order's headquarters, makes a space undetectable and untraceable?
questions.tidbits: Once the Fidelius Charm is cast, only the Secret-Keeper can reveal the location — even the inhabitants cannot betray it.

questions.questionText: How many players are on each Quidditch team?
questions.tidbits: Each team has three Chasers, two Beaters, one Keeper, and one Seeker.

questions.questionText: How many points is catching the Golden Snitch worth in Quidditch?
questions.tidbits: The team whose Seeker catches the Snitch also ends the game, though catching it doesn't guarantee a win if the score gap is large.

questions.questionText: What are the four Hogwarts houses?
questions.tidbits: Each house values different traits: bravery, loyalty, wit, and ambition respectively.

questions.questionText: What magical creature form does Professor Lupin take during the full moon?
questions.tidbits: Lupin takes Wolfsbane Potion to remain in control during transformations, though it does not prevent the change itself.

questions.questionText: What animal does Harry's Patronus take the form of?
questions.tidbits: Harry's Patronus is a stag because his father James Potter's Animagus form was also a stag — Harry inherited this connection.

questions.questionText: What are the two standardized wizarding exams students take at Hogwarts, one at the end of fifth year and one at the end of seventh year?
questions.answerText: O.W.L.s (Ordinary Wizarding Levels) and N.E.W.T.s (Nastily Exhausting Wizarding Tests)
questions.tidbits: Achieving an 'Outstanding' on a N.E.W.T. is required to pursue careers like Auror or Healer.

questions.questionText: What are the four balls used in a game of Quidditch?
questions.answerText: The Quaffle, two Bludgers, and the Golden Snitch
questions.tidbits: Bludgers are enchanted iron balls that actively pursue players; Beaters use bats to deflect them.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the magical law that prevents underage wizards from using magic outside of school?
questions.answerText: The Decree for the Reasonable Restriction of Underage Sorcery
questions.tidbits: The Ministry detects magic by area, not by individual — meaning magic used by adult wizards in a home with a child can be misattributed.

questions.questionText: What branch of magic governs the behavior of magical creatures, including dragons, hippogriffs, and merpeople, in wizarding law?
questions.answerText: The Department for the Regulation and Control of Magical Creatures
questions.tidbits: Hagrid's Care of Magical Creatures classes often feature creatures that the Ministry classifies as 'dangerous' or 'XXXXX.'

questions.questionText: What is the incantation for the Killing Curse, and what is the only known counter — not a spell, but a force?
questions.tidbits: No spell can block Avada Kedavra directly — only the protective enchantment born of a loving sacrifice can deflect it.

questions.questionText: What position does Harry Potter play on the Gryffindor Quidditch team?
questions.tidbits: Harry became the youngest Seeker in a century when Professor McGonagall spotted his flying ability in first year.

questions.questionText: What international tournament involving the three largest European wizarding schools does Harry compete in during his fourth year?
questions.tidbits: The Triwizard Tournament was discontinued after several deaths, then revived for the events of Goblet of Fire.

questions.questionText: What magical object sorts new Hogwarts students into their houses at the start of term?
questions.tidbits: The Sorting Hat was once a normal hat belonging to Godric Gryffindor, enchanted by the four founders to carry out sorting.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the student defense group Harry leads in his fifth year, formed in secret to resist Umbridge's ban on practical Defense Against the Dark Arts?
questions.tidbits: Dumbledore's Army meets in the Room of Requirement, a room that appears only when a person has genuine need of it.

questions.questionText: What position does Oliver Wood play on the Gryffindor Quidditch team?
questions.tidbits: Oliver Wood is obsessively passionate about Quidditch, giving exhaustive pre-match tactical speeches to his teammates.

questions.questionText: In which Quidditch World Cup final do Ireland beat Bulgaria, and who is Bulgaria's star Seeker?
questions.tidbits: Viktor Krum catches the Golden Snitch but Bulgaria still loses because Ireland led by too many points.

questions.questionText: What award does Harry win in his first year after breaking school rules — flying on his broomstick without permission?
questions.answerText: A spot on the Gryffindor Quidditch team as Seeker
questions.tidbits: Professor McGonagall bends the rules forbidding first-years from having their own broomsticks to put Harry on the team.

questions.questionText: What are the four Hogwarts house points totals typically displayed in the hourglasses in the entrance hall, and what house wins the House Cup most often during Harry's time?
questions.answerText: Gryffindor wins the House Cup in Harry's first, third, and later years; Slytherin had a long winning streak before that
questions.tidbits: Slytherin had won the House Cup seven years in a row before Dumbledore awarded last-minute points to Gryffindor in Harry's first year.

questions.questionText: What Quidditch foul involves a player grabbing another player's broomstick tail to slow them down?
questions.tidbits: There are 700 known Quidditch fouls, all of which reportedly occurred in the 1473 World Cup.

questions.questionText: In the Triwizard Tournament's second task, what do champions use to breathe underwater, and what does Harry use?
questions.answerText: Cedric used a bubble-head charm, Fleur used a bubble-head charm, Krum partially transfigured himself — Harry used gillyweed
questions.tidbits: Dobby steals the gillyweed from Snape's personal stores to help Harry after Neville accidentally provides the idea.
